items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.id: PolyShell (no CVE/APSB ID provided by source)
items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw, named PolyShell, in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, potentially leading to remote code execution and account takeover. Mass exploitation attempts have been observed.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source to protect against multiple vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, opening the door to remote code execution.

items.title: Amasty patches 25 Magento extensions, one critical
items.summary: Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield reportedly blocks the critical issues.
